hits.title: Fine-Tuning and Deploying LLMs on Mobile:F/b to learnings

story_text.value:
There has been a few posts about LM Link here on HN, but I don't believe this has gotten the attention it deserves.

https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47155258

Finally got around to dusting off a old setup and I am thrilled with the results. I'm using a 200$ used GPU and 11year old CPU. Basically a retired game rig. I can now host a private model to my entire family that is just as capable as the things they were using it for without worrying of all the private information leaking out. While I am extremely impressed on the outputs of the local model, I am not to concerned about its quality. It's fine. What's more exciting is it's a path forward to data sovereignty and ownership.

Great job on the privacy policy.

https://locallyai.app/privacy-policy.html

Setup was easy.

As hardware improves, and local models improve...this is the way.

The connection is powered by tailscale, long-term I would like to see other implementations of this wire to diversify how these devices are connecting.

Iroh, recently posted here comes to mind... But in general, a stream of text is not a heavy payload and shouldnt need to rely on a loss-leader from any one place.

title.value: LM Link – This is the future I want

hits.author: smalltorch

story_text.value:
ChatGPT 3.5 only came out about three and a half years ago. Claude Code was released in February 2025. In one academic year, LLMs went from barely handling high school math to disproving unit-distance conjecture.

Every two months we seems to have a new SOTA now. What do you expect AI coding to look like four years from now?

Will it be like Go after AlphaGo, where even Ke Jie could only say "they no longer knew what they were fighting against"? Or will it just be like today, just stronger, with humans still driving the work and AI as a powerful assistant?

title.value: Ask HN: What will AI coding look like when today's CS freshmen graduate?

hits.author: linzhangrun
